name: tb-impl
description: TB implementation: read MAS DV Plan → test sequences → TB → sim loop → coverage

tasks.detail: Read <module>_mas.md. From §2 extract port list. From §4 extract register map (offsets, bitfields, access types). From §5 extract interrupt sources + clear mechanism. From §6 extract memory configurations. From §9 extract all test sequences (S1-SN table), coverage goals, SVA assertions, and corner cases. List everything — do not write code yet.

Use /gen-tc <module>. Implement EVERY sequence from MAS §9 Test Sequence table (S1-SN): S1 reset check, S2 basic operation, S3 interrupt flow (enable → trigger → check irq → W1C clear → verify irq deasserts), S4 memory R/W (write pattern → readback → compare), S5 back-to-back, S6 error injection, S7 corner cases. Each test increments pass_cnt or fail_cnt.

tasks.content: Add SVA assertions (from MAS §9 coverage goals)
tasks.detail: Add all SVA assertions listed in MAS §9. Key assertions: (1) FSM no illegal state transitions, (2) irq must deassert within 1 cycle after W1C clear, (3) FIFO never overflow/underflow, (4) register reset values correct after rst_n. Bind assertions in TB.

Include: `include tc_<module>.sv, DUT instantiation with all ports from MAS §2, clock gen (always #5 clk=~clk), reset sequence matching MAS §7 timing, call all tc_ tasks in S1-SN order, print final pass/fail count, $finish.
Run /sim tb_<module>.sv. If fail: check [FAIL] lines, diagnose DUT vs TB bug. Fix TB bugs here; DUT bugs → [MAS ESCALATE] rtl-gen. Verify all S1-SN sequences show [PASS].

tasks.content: Coverage review: meet MAS §9 coverage goals
tasks.detail: Run /coverage <module>. Check: line ≥ 90%, branch ≥ 85%, toggle ≥ 80%. Verify all FSM states visited, all interrupt sources fired+cleared, all register bits toggled. Add tc_ tasks for gaps. Re-run sim if new TCs added.
